evidence_quotes.quote: Games that are purchased and/or are owned by a consumer have to stay available to play in the state when it was released and/or when the online services/servers were being shut down by the publisher/studio of the game.
You should be able to own the game, whether it is digital or physical.

paragraphs.text: There should be a section added for games. Games that are purchased and/or are owned by a consumer have to stay available to play in the state when it was released and/or when the online services/servers were being shut down by the publisher/studio of the game. For example, The Crew (by Ubisoft). This isn't a subscription service for a game. You should be able to own the game, whether it is digital or physical. However, they make it impossible to keep playing the game whenever you want after a couple of years. This has to change.
